conventions.id_style: Uppercase namespace segments separated by dots; stable public IDs do not embed package-release version numbers.
conventions.stability: Layer-1 registry IDs are the slow-changing public vocabulary. Layer-2 seed IDs are public within a release but may evolve across governed releases.
conventions.registration_policy: Only IDs declared under a registered namespace in this file belong to the Core Semantic Package vocabulary. Unregistered additions are local or provisional until promoted in a later governed release.

namespaces.meaning: Governed cross-paradigm operator-family identifiers that inhabit an edge slot.
Canonical Compass dimension identifiers (Layer 1 semantic hierarchy aligned to the eight Compasses).
Canonical machine-control identifiers (validation strictness, provenance mode, and similar non-semantic controls).

notes: Registered namespaces define the shared public vocabulary of the Core Semantic Package.
If a downstream user introduces a new local field, family, or runtime handle that is not registered here, it should be treated as local/provisional rather than as part of the canonical ontology.
Edge slot IDs are purely structural directed pairs. Edge-family IDs add semantic operator roles and may evolve across releases through governance.
This Core Semantic Package intentionally does not register runtime-projection namespaces such as knob trees or auto-recognition signatures; those belong in faster-moving companion materials.
